author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
hari he!
tomāra caraṇapadma, anurāga⋅sudhāsadma⋅,
sāgaraśīkara jadi pāya
kona bhāgyavān jane, kona kārya⋅saŋghaṭane,
tā’ra saba duḥkha dūre jāya

se⋅sudhā⋅samudrakaṇa, saṁsārāgni⋅nirvāpaṇa,
kṣaṇeke kariyā phele tā’ra
parama⋅nirvṛti diyā, tomāra caraṇe lañā,
deya tabe ānanda apāra

e bhaktivinoda kā̐de, paḍiyā saṁsāra⋅phā̐de,
bale, nāhi kona bhāgya mora
e ghaṭanā nā ghaṭila, āmāra janama gela,
vṛthā rainu ha’ye ātmabhora
en_translation:
Hari! Your lotus feet, a dwelling of the nectar of divine love, if a fortunate soul obtains a drop from that ocean, through some course of action, all his sorrows vanish.

That particle of the ocean of nectar, extinguishes the fire of material existence, it does so in an instant. Giving supreme peace, taking him to Your feet, it then bestows boundless joy.

This Bhaktivinoda cries, having fallen into the trap of material existence, says, "I have no fortune. This event of receiving that nectar did not occur, my life has passed, I remained uselessly engrossed in myself."
